nginx.name: NGINX
nginx.role: Reverse proxy, static file serving, HTTPS termination

github-actions.name: GitHub Actions
github-actions.role: CI/CD pipeline — lint, build, test, deploy

react-core.name: React.js
react-core.role: Main UI library for component-based architecture

react-patterns.title: React Best Practices
body: Component-based architecture with reusable UI units.
Prefer functional components with hooks.
Keep components small and feature-oriented.
Avoid unnecessary re-renders using memoization.
Use composition over inheritance.

zustand.title: Zustand
body: Use for simple, fast, and minimal global state needs.
Keep stores small and focused on a single domain or feature.
Prefer it for UI state, client-side interactions, and local app logic.
Avoid using it as a replacement for server-state management tools.
Leverage selectors to derive computed state instead of duplicating state.
Keep TypeScript strict to maintain predictable state shape.

redux.title: Redux
body: Use when the application requires strict structure and predictable state flow.
Prefer for large-scale applications with complex shared state.
Organize logic with slices, reducers, and actions using Redux Toolkit.
Centralize state changes to ensure traceability and debugging clarity.
Use DevTools actively to inspect state changes and flows.
Avoid overusing Redux for simple or local UI state.

tanstack-query.title: TanStack Query
body: Use for all server-state (API data), never for UI state.
Treat the cache as the source of truth for remote data.
Let it handle caching, retries, refetching, and loading states by default.
Design query keys carefully and keep them stable and predictable.
Use mutations for all write operations (create/update/delete).
Do not duplicate server-state in local state unless strictly necessary.

zod.title: Zod
body: Define schemas first, then infer TypeScript types from them.
Use it as the single source of truth for validation logic.
Validate all external inputs (API, forms, user data) at the boundary.
Fail fast on invalid data instead of silently coercing state.
Keep schemas modular and reusable across frontend and backend.

tanstack-table.title: TanStack Table
body: Use as a headless engine: you fully control the UI layer.
Keep logic (sorting, filtering, pagination) separate from rendering.
Design tables as composable building blocks, not monolithic components.
Optimize for large datasets with memoization and virtualization when needed.
Treat table state as external state when building complex dashboards.

sweetalert2.title: SweetAlert2
body: Use for quick, consistent feedback UX (confirmations, alerts, toasts).
Keep dialogs simple and action-focused.
Avoid overusing modals for flows that should be full pages.
Standardize UX patterns across the app for consistency.
Trigger only from explicit user actions, not side effects.

react-icons.name: react-icons
react-icons.role: Use as a unified icon system; keep icons consistent, minimal, and semantic across the UI

react-performance.title: React Performance
body: Use React.lazy + Suspense for all routes and heavy components to reduce initial bundle size.
Rely on TanStack Query caching to avoid unnecessary API refetching and redundant network calls.
Optimize images by using WebP or AVIF formats, and always define width and height to prevent layout shifts.
Avoid importing entire libraries; only import what is needed to ensure proper tree-shaking and smaller bundles.

performance.title: Performance
body: Memoization to avoid unnecessary renders.
Optimize API calls with caching.
Use lightweight dependencies.
Prefer modular architecture.

security.title: Security (OWASP)
body: Validate all inputs.
Prevent XSS and injection attacks.
Secure authentication flows.
Never use `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` without explicit sanitization.
Never log tokens, passwords, or PII. No sensitive data in unencrypted `localStorage`.
Use HTTPS in production.
